methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nuclearn.ai is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised $10.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 nuclear energy peers: three public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and eight private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. This narrow peer set reflects the capital-intensive, early-stage nature of the frontier nuclear sector.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach, using the $10.5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next funding stage. This method yields $21M. Peer-median valuation cannot be reliably applied: while eight same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables produces fewer than three datapoints, making the median statistically unstable. An all-sector fallback would introduce unacceptable distortion given nuclear energy's distinct capital requirements and risk profile.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $18M to $24M, with a median of $21M. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, path to regulatory approval, and capital efficiency relative to the identified peer group. The single-method foundation of this analysis underscores the difficulty in valuing frontier nuclear ventures; investors should treat this range as indicative pending broader peer data availability or company-specific operational milestones.
